proven.claim: PROVEN: When --signal-model flag is provided, llmModel equals that value
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3, P6
; LINE: 529
; PROVEN: When --signal-model flag is provided, llmModel equals that value
(declare-const signal_model_flag String)
(declare-const default_model String)
(declare-const llm_model String)
(assert (= signal_model_flag "claude-sonnet"))
(assert (= default_model "sonnet"))
(assert (= llm_model signal_model_flag))
(assert (= llm_model "claude-sonnet"))
(assert (not (= llm_model "claude-sonnet")))
(check-sat)
; unsat → llmModel correctly uses --signal-model flag value

proven.claim: REACHABLE: Multiple calls accumulate generators instead of replacing
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P2, P4
; LINE: 529
; REACHABLE: Multiple calls accumulate generators instead of replacing
; Temporal analysis: first call registers generator A, second call adds generator B
(declare-const generators_after_first Int)
(declare-const generators_after_second Int)
(declare-const model_first String)
(declare-const model_second String)
(assert (= generators_after_first 3))
(assert (= model_first "sonnet"))
(assert (= generators_after_second (+ generators_after_first 1)))
(assert (= model_second "claude"))
(assert (= generators_after_second 4))
; Log from second call: "LLM signal generator enabled (model: claude)"
; But registry now has BOTH sonnet AND claude generators
(assert (= generators_after_second generators_after_first))
(check-sat)
; sat → both generators are accumulated, log only reports last model's generator

proven.claim: PROVEN: registry.register() adds the LLMSignalGenerator to the registry
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2, P3
; LINE: 529
; PROVEN: registry.register() adds the LLMSignalGenerator to the registry
; State transition: initial generators + 1 = final generators
(declare-const initial_generator_count Int)
(declare-const generator_count_after_register Int)
(assert (= initial_generator_count 3))
(assert (= generator_count_after_register (+ initial_generator_count 1)))
(assert (= generator_count_after_register 4))
(assert (not (= generator_count_after_register 4)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → register() correctly adds the generator to the registry

violations.claim: REACHABLE: --signal-model accepts any string without validation
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P3, P5
; LINE: 529
; REACHABLE: --signal-model accepts any string without validation
; No validation that model name is supported by any provider
(declare-const signal_model_flag String)
(declare-const llm_model String)
(declare-const is_valid_provider_model Bool)
(assert (= signal_model_flag "nonexistent-model-xyz"))
(assert (= llm_model signal_model_flag))
(assert (= is_valid_provider_model false))
; Log accurately outputs the model name provided by user
; But LLMSignalGenerator may fail at runtime when it tries to use invalid model
(check-sat)
; sat → invalid model names are accepted without validation, may cause downstream failures
